[189:02]And Michael D'Amino, not only is he not a neocon, he's quite the opposite.

[189:21]We use terms like neocons, interventionists.

[189:24]We talk about non-interventionists, isolationists.

[189:36]I don't think many of them are neoconservatives, strictly speaking.

[189:41]And the opponents are actually not non-interventionists per se.

[189:46]I don't think that Donald Trump is a non-interventionist.

[189:57]He's in favor of a restrained foreign policy.

[190:01]Donald Trump does intervene.

[190:03]Donald Trump does operate as a global policeman.

[190:07]He does persecute an American foreign policy, or prosecute an American foreign policy around

[190:13]the world in Venezuela, in Iran, in North Korea, in Russia, everywhere.

[190:26]forces, mercenaries. He does so in other ways with energy politics. He does so with sanctions.

[190:34]He does so with bilateral trade deals. That is how he conducts a foreign policy. It's

[190:48]But it's with a degree of restraint in using force.

[190:52]And so this guy that was appointed in the DoD, Michael D'Amino, who will now head up

[190:57]Middle East policy in the DoD, he is an advocate for this US policy of restraint.
